source_intake_record.raw_question_text: As shown in the figure, the rhombus $ABCD$ has a side length of $10$, and $\angle A=60^\circ$. By successively connecting the midpoints of each side of the rhombus $ABCD$, quadrilateral ${A}_{1}{B}_{1}{C}_{1}{D}_{1}$ is obtained; by successively connecting the midpoints of each side of quadrilateral ${A}_{1}{B}_{1}{C}_{1}{D}_{1}$, quadrilateral ${A}_{2}{B}_{2}{C}_{2}{D}_{2}$ is obtained; by successively connecting the midpoints of each side of quadrilateral ${A}_{2}{B}_{2}{C}_{2}{D}_{2}$, quadrilateral ${A}_{3}{B}_{3}{C}_{3}{D}_{3}$ is obtained; and so on. Following this pattern, what is the perimeter of the quadrilateral ${A}_{2n}{B}_{2n}{C}_{2n}{D}_{2n}$?
source_intake_record.raw_answer_text:
\textbf{Solution:} By utilizing the properties of mid-quadrilaterals, we know quadrilaterals $A_{1}B_{1}C_{1}D_{1}$ and $A_{3}B_{3}C_{3}D_{3}$ are rectangles, while $A_{2}B_{2}C_{2}D_{2}$ and $A_{4}B_{4}C_{4}D_{4}$ are rhombuses.\\
Since the side length of rhombus ABCD is 10, and $\angle A=60^\circ$,\\
therefore, $\triangle ADB$ and $\triangle AA_{1}D_{1}$ are equilateral triangles, and the perimeter of rhombus ABCD is $10\times 4=40$;\\
therefore, the perimeter of rhombus $A_{2}B_{2}C_{2}D_{2}$ is $\frac{1}{2}\times 40=20$;\\
The perimeter of rhombus $A_{4}B_{4}C_{4}D_{4}$ is $\frac{1}{{2}^{2}}\times 40$;\\
$ \dots $\\
therefore, the perimeter of quadrilateral ${A}_{2n}{B}_{2n}{C}_{2n}{D}_{2n}$ is $40\times \frac{1}{{2}^{n}}=5\times 8\times \frac{1}{{2}^{n}}=\boxed{\frac{5}{{2}^{n-3}}}$.

solving likely involves more than one step: deriving the first transformed perimeter and then generalizing to the 2n-th stage
question_text is relatively long and describes a sequence of constructions rather than a single direct lookup
metadata.multi_solution_policy.should_push_multi_solution_agent=true suggests the sample is considered to have nontrivial reasoning potential upstream
verifiability: the target output is a single closed-form perimeter expression, which is easier to check than an open-ended explanation
answer_present=true and registry_passed=true indicate the sample is complete and registered successfully
the geometry transformation described in the text supports rule-based or symbolic checking of whether the perimeter halves in the required pattern

by successively connecting the midpoints of each side of quadrilateral ${A}_{1}{B}_{1}{C}_{1}{D}_{1}$, quadrilateral ${A}_{2}{B}_{2}{C}_{2}{D}_{2}$ is obtained
by successively connecting the midpoints of each side of quadrilateral ${A}_{2}{B}_{2}{C}_{2}{D}_{2}$, quadrilateral ${A}_{3}{B}_{3}{C}_{3}{D}_{3}$ is obtained
Following this pattern, what is the perimeter of the quadrilateral ${A}_{2n}{B}_{2n}{C}_{2n}{D}_{2n}$?

node_records.canonical_value: Following this pattern, what is the perimeter of the quadrilateral ${A}_{2n}{B}_{2n}{C}_{2n}{D}_{2n}$?
surface_forms: Following this pattern, what is the perimeter of the quadrilateral ${A}_{2n}{B}_{2n}{C}_{2n}{D}_{2n}$?

normalized_value.target_text: Following this pattern, what is the perimeter of the quadrilateral ${A}_{2n}{B}_{2n}{C}_{2n}{D}_{2n}$?
normalized_value.expected_answer:
\textbf{Solution:} By utilizing the properties of mid-quadrilaterals, we know quadrilaterals $A_{1}B_{1}C_{1}D_{1}$ and $A_{3}B_{3}C_{3}D_{3}$ are rectangles, while $A_{2}B_{2}C_{2}D_{2}$ and $A_{4}B_{4}C_{4}D_{4}$ are rhombuses.\\
Since the side length of rhombus ABCD is 10, and $\angle A=60^\circ$,\\
therefore, $\triangle ADB$ and $\triangle AA_{1}D_{1}$ are equilateral triangles, and the perimeter of rhombus ABCD is $10\times 4=40$;\\
therefore, the perimeter of rhombus $A_{2}B_{2}C_{2}D_{2}$ is $\frac{1}{2}\times 40=20$;\\
The perimeter of rhombus $A_{4}B_{4}C_{4}D_{4}$ is $\frac{1}{{2}^{2}}\times 40$;\\
$\dots$\\
therefore, the perimeter of quadrilateral ${A}_{2n}{B}_{2n}{C}_{2n}{D}_{2n}$ is $40\times \frac{1}{{2}^{n}}=5\times 8\times \frac{1}{{2}^{n}}=\boxed{\frac{5}{{2}^{n-3}}}$.

\textbf{Solution:} By utilizing the properties of mid-quadrilaterals, we know quadrilaterals $A_{1}B_{1}C_{1}D_{1}$ and $A_{3}B_{3}C_{3}D_{3}$ are rectangles, while $A_{2}B_{2}C_{2}D_{2}$ and $A_{4}B_{4}C_{4}D_{4}$ are rhombuses.\\

therefore, the perimeter of rhombus $A_{2}B_{2}C_{2}D_{2}$ is $\frac{1}{2}\times 40=20$;\\
The perimeter of rhombus $A_{4}B_{4}C_{4}D_{4}$ is $\frac{1}{{2}^{2}}\times 40$;\\

therefore, the perimeter of quadrilateral ${A}_{2n}{B}_{2n}{C}_{2n}{D}_{2n}$ is $40\times \frac{1}{{2}^{n}}=5\times 8\times \frac{1}{{2}^{n}}=\boxed{\frac{5}{{2}^{n-3}}}$.
